sweep_metadata.notes: Day 81 priority scope: (1) Trump 'scheduled attack for tomorrow' (May 19) called off at Gulf state troika request — highest operationalization of US kinetic action since ceasefire; (2) Barakah nuclear plant drone strike May 17 — first attack on UAE nuclear-adjacent infrastructure; directly challenges Cooper Tier-1 proxy-connectivity claim; (3) Netanyahu-Trump call May 17 — breaks 7-day Penetration-mechanism dormancy; trilateral call adds Qatar PM; (4) Iran submits revised 14-point text via Pakistan + US proposes temporary oil sanctions waiver; (5) Knesset dissolution preliminary vote expected May 20-21 (not yet occurred); (6) Brent pullback from $111+ band-break to ~$102 on deal-signal cluster. Multiple triggers fired this cycle; thesis holding with structural elaboration required on Gulf state troika brake mechanism.

finding_cards.finding: Barakah nuclear plant drone strike May 17: three drones entered UAE from western border; two intercepted by UAE air defenses; one struck electrical generator outside inner perimeter. No radiation leak; one reactor on emergency diesel generators per IAEA. No party officially claimed responsibility; UAE investigating. JPost (two unnamed sources, −50% discount): 'In any case, it's Iran who ordered the attack.' IRGC spokesperson Zolfaghari (Tier-2 equivalent, named): 'All US military bases in the southern Persian Gulf will be deactivated'; 'In the past 24 hours the IRGC Navy did not allow any vessel to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.' The Zolfaghari threat is the most aggressive IRGC named-principal statement of the cycle. No Vahidi direct statement. Aliabadi silent. Iran's revised 14-point text submitted via Pakistan (Tasnim/Xinhua T3 corroborated) indicates Ghalibaf-Araghchi diplomatic track still operative. CRITICAL: Barakah attack directly contradicts Cooper Tier-1 Senate testimony (May 14): 'Hamas, Hezbollah, and Houthis are all cut off from Iran's weapons and support.' The western-border drone entry points to Houthi (Yemen) or Iraqi militia launch. Either Cooper's assessment is outdated (8 days elapsed) or the Houthi indigenous-manufacturing capability (documented by FP March 2026: airframe/warhead local assembly, guidance/propulsion still Iran-supplied) is not fully severed. Cooper discount warranted on proxy-connectivity claim.

trigger_detail.appendix_b_row: Vahidi direct named statement (not fired); Cooper proxy-connectivity Tier-1 claim challenged by Barakah attack (PROBE-14 cross-reference); horizontal escalation to nuclear-adjacent Gulf infrastructure (new threshold)
trigger_detail.framework_variable: BS-13 Iranian residual capability; BS-8 IRGC factional posture; Layer 2 asymmetric-conflict calibration
trigger_detail.direction: up — proxy capability demonstration despite Cooper T1 claim; IRGC escalation posture (Zolfaghari); but simultaneous diplomatic track (revised 14-point text) maintains Ghalibaf-Araghchi agency
trigger_detail.gap_to_full_match: Vahidi direct named statement. Official attribution of Barakah attack with sourcing above T3. Confirmation Cooper assessment outdated vs proxy connectivity genuinely severed but Houthi acting autonomously.

discounts_applied: -50% on JPost attribution sourcing (two unnamed sources)
-50% on IRGC Zolfaghari 'all bases deactivated' — threat statement, not operational order
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Cooper Tier-1 testimony (May 14): 'Houthis cut off from Iran's weapons and support.' Barakah attack (May 17): drones from western border, pattern consistent with Houthi or Iraqi militia origin. Direct conflict at confidence level: Cooper's T1 claim is the strongest sourcing; Barakah attack is the operational contradiction. Resolution options: (a) Cooper was accurate on May 14 but Houthi capability was already indigenous (pre-existing manufacturing); (b) Cooper overstated the severance; (c) Iranian support was partially reconstituted in 8 days. Framework: apply −25% confidence on Cooper proxy-connectivity claim; treat Houthi/Iraqi militia as partially intact Iranian extended deterrent pending attribution confirmation.

finding_cards.finding: USS Gerald R. Ford confirmed home at Norfolk as of May 16-17 per War Zone (T3, authoritative fleet tracker) and CNN (T2). Ford completed a record 326-day deployment including Operation Epic Fury / Absolute Resolve, transited Suez northbound May 1, and returned home May 16. This resolves the Day 79 CENTCOM synthesis error: the Day 79 synthesis flagged 'Truman (CSG-10)' as the departing CSG but the departing carrier was the Ford. Synthesis three-CSG baseline was Lincoln + Bush + Ford, not Lincoln + Bush + Truman. Two-CSG posture (Lincoln + Bush) confirmed with H confidence across War Zone fleet tracker (authoritative). No Eisenhower deployment order. No ROE change. No new operation name. No DOE/SOF nuclear-expertise forward deployment signal. STRUCTURAL: Trump announcing a 'scheduled attack for tomorrow' (May 18 Truth Social) and then postponing it confirms that US kinetic resumption had moved from planning to execution-ready stage — military machinery was further advanced than Day 79 synthesis priced. The postponement at Gulf state request does not change the underlying operational readiness.

Eisenhower non-deployment restraint signal marginally elevated given two-CSG (vs three-CSG) baseline.

finding_cards.finding: Brent reversed from Day 79 band-break ($111.04 intraday May 15) to approximately $102 range on May 18 as two deal-signal drivers hit simultaneously: (1) Trump announcing postponement of 'scheduled attack for tomorrow' (May 19) — kinetic-resumption premium partially reversed; (2) Iranian media (Tasnim) reporting US proposed temporary oil sanctions waiver, corroborated by Bloomberg meta-reporting (Bloomberg reporting that Tasnim reported; US has not confirmed). Brent returned to within the $102-110 A1-oscillation band from the top. Concurrent physical-tightness driver: Zolfaghari (IRGC spokesperson) stated IRGC Navy 'did not allow any vessel to pass through the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours' — Hormuz enforcement language hardening even as diplomatic signals soften. Two-track Hormuz equilibrium: Iranian-language Hormuz enforcement concurrent with deal signal is structurally complex. Hapag-Lloyd/Maersk commercial restoration: not announced. Brent scenario interpretation: $102 pullback from above-band position is a partial normalization toward deal premium; not yet at $95 Fork B credibility trigger; remains in upper portion of the A1-oscillation band.

Market is pricing deal probability (attack postponed; text submitted; waiver proposed) while physical closure continues. The conflict is between the diplomatic signal layer and the physical-constraint layer — structurally this is the Fearon-mechanism playing out: signals of deal ease the premium while the coercive physical state persists.

finding_cards.finding: Knesset dissolution preliminary vote NOT occurred as of May 18. Expected vote date: May 20 or 21 (JNS/ToI T2). Coalition filed bill May 13; four plenum readings required. Haredi parties (Degel HaTorah — spiritual leader Dov Lando) expressing distrust of Netanyahu's promises on draft-exemption bill (Haaretz May 18 T2); this is the internal coalition stress driver of the dissolution push. If the haredi bloc votes dissolution, passage is 'all but assured' (Day 79 ToI baseline holds). Netanyahu-Trump phone call May 17 confirmed (ITV, ToI T2; ~30 minutes, focused on 'possibility of renewing fighting in Iran'). Netanyahu convened security cabinet May 17 — meeting about renewed Iran strikes per Israeli media. White House official X post (T1): Trump hosted trilateral call with Netanyahu AND Qatar PM Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani — Qatar is operating simultaneously as (a) Gulf state persuader asking Trump to hold attack (Emir Tamim was among the three Gulf leaders) and (b) trilateral diplomatic bridge between US and Israel. Netanyahu statement: 'our eyes are also open; prepared for any scenario.' No documented Israeli strike Day 80-81. Israeli pre-emption threshold: consistent with Day 79 baseline but Netanyahu engaged in de-escalation diplomacy (trilateral call) concurrently with 'prepared for any scenario' framing.

The postponement temporarily preserves the constitutional constraint window. No new Republican defection signal. No judicial WPA challenge filed. Stage 2 hysteresis indicators accumulating: 'hostilities terminated' certification, Day 60 WPA deadline passed, operation-rebranding mechanism operative. Fetterman mechanism unchanged.

finding_cards.finding: Iran submitted revised 14-point text to Pakistani mediator May 18 (Tasnim T3 + Xinhua T3; no direct US-side confirmation). The revised text is described as focused on 'negotiations to end the war and confidence-building measures by the American side' — structurally more conciliatory framing than prior rejection-of-'totally-unacceptable' US proposal. Key Iranian revision in revised text: HEU to be 'diluted and rest transferred to a third country' (modifying prior demand structure); enrichment suspension shorter than 20-year US moratorium; 30-day timeline focal point retained. US reportedly proposed temporary oil sanctions waiver (Tasnim; Bloomberg meta-reporting; US unconfirmed). Trump told Gulf leaders deal is 'very close' as justification for 2-3 day postponement (ToI T2). Pakistan mediating this round (not Oman); Pakistan channel is newly active. No Oman FM 2026 post-war round announcement. The Meta-Negotiation Regression pattern is interrupted: active diplomatic exchange is occurring (revised text submitted; waiver proposed; 2-3 day window framed as deal-productive). This does not confirm Fork B is operative; it confirms the negotiating mechanism is active within a compressed window.

Weight private channel activity (Pakistan-mediated revised text) more than public framing in Araghchi's BRICS speech.

finding_cards.finding: FRAMEWORK REVISION TRIGGER. Trump announced on Truth Social (Tier 1) May 18 that a military attack on Iran scheduled for 'tomorrow' (May 19) was postponed at the request of three Gulf leaders: Emir of Qatar Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, and UAE President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan. Trump stated: leaders believed 'a deal would be made that would be very acceptable to the US and its allies'; Trump warned military is prepared for 'full large-scale assault of Iran' if acceptable deal not reached. This is the highest operationalization of US kinetic action confirmed since the ceasefire: a specific date was set for military resumption, then postponed under Gulf state pressure. Trump's 48h framing has compressed to a 2-3 day deal window (per ToI T2 interpretation of Gulf leader messaging). White House (T1): trilateral call Trump + Netanyahu + Qatar PM Abdulrahman Al Thani May 17 — Qatar simultaneously brokering the Gulf state holdoff (Emir Tamim) and the US-Israel diplomatic architecture (PM Abdulrahman in trilateral with Netanyahu). Netanyahu-Trump call May 17 (ITV, ToI T2): breaks 7-day Penetration-mechanism dormancy; call focused on 'possibility of renewing fighting in Iran'; Trump briefed Netanyahu on China visit. Trump concurrent statements: 'clock is ticking'; 'won't be anything left of them if they don't get moving FAST'; 'Iranians should be afraid'; simultaneously: 'Iran still wants a deal'; 'deal very close' (per Gulf leaders). A1 oscillation at maximum severity: simultaneous attack-scheduling (hardline), attack postponement (deal-leaning), hardline rhetoric ('annihilation'), and deal-optimism ('very close'). Iranian PA-gap: no Vahidi direct statement; Ghalibaf-Araghchi track active (revised 14-point text submitted). NEW STRUCTURAL VARIABLE: Gulf state troika (Saudi/UAE/Qatar) functioning as direct brake on US military decision-making — not modeled in existing PA-gap framework. This is the first documented case of Gulf state leaders directly intervening in the US military decision cycle for Iran kinetics.

trigger_detail.appendix_b_row: Multiple triggers: (1) Trump attack-scheduling = US Fork A execution-ready (highest operationalization to date); (2) Gulf state troika brake = new unmodeled constraint mechanism; (3) 2-3 day decision window = specific fork-selection timeline; (4) Netanyahu-Penetration mechanism reactivated (7-day dormancy broken)
trigger_detail.framework_variable: Layer 4 principal-access-channel architecture; Layer 5 PA-gap; Fork A operationalization state; BS-15 first-mover architecture
trigger_detail.direction: complex — Fork A operationalization CONFIRMED at execution-ready stage (attack scheduled); simultaneously Fork B-multilateral gaining traction via Gulf state brake and Iranian revised text; net effect: both Fork A and Fork B probabilities have structural anchors; decision window compressed to 2-3 days

variable_moved.variable: US Fork A operationalization state; Gulf state troika constraint; Netanyahu-Penetration mechanism; A1 oscillation severity
variable_moved.prior_value: Day 79: Trump hardline ('deal or annihilate'); attack not scheduled; Netanyahu-Penetration dormant 7 days; Fork A 28-38% 30d
variable_moved.new_value: Day 81: Attack scheduled for May 19 then postponed — US Fork A was at execution-ready state, not just 'operationalizing.' Gulf state troika (Saudi/UAE/Qatar) introduced as new unmodeled constraint variable that directly halted US military action. Netanyahu-Penetration reactivated (7-day dormancy broken; trilateral Qatar-bridge added). 2-3 day window = deal-or-military decision by approximately May 20-21. Fork A probability for 30d: maintained or marginally reduced (attack postponed; deal window active) but the underlying execution-ready state is structural — attack can be rescheduled if deal fails. Fork B-multilateral probability: elevated from Day 79 baseline (Gulf state troika as new multilateral mechanism; Iranian revised text; deal framed as 'very close'). Proposed synthesis revision: add Gulf state troika as §5.25 mechanism — third-party Gulf state constraint on US military decision, operating independently of Israeli alliance coordination and Chinese co-mediation.

discounts_applied: -0% on Trump Truth Social announcement (T1, self-disclosure, multi-source corroborated)
-25% on '2-3 day window' framing (Trump paraphrase of Gulf leader messaging; not a formal deadline)
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Trump 'annihilation' rhetoric (hardline) vs Trump postponing attack (deal-leaning) in the same 24h arc. This is not a contradiction; it is A1 oscillation at maximum severity. The constraint architecture explanation: Gulf state pressure (new variable) is the operative brake; it does not eliminate Trump's underlying hardline disposition, it delays its expression. The 2-3 day window is the interval in which either deal signal arrives and Trump accepts the brake outcome, or no deal signal arrives and Trump reactivates the military decision. Structural: the Gulf state brake mechanism is the first external (non-US, non-Israeli, non-Iranian) direct constraint on US military timing. This is a new Layer 4 variable.

finding_cards.finding: FRAMEWORK REVISION TRIGGER. Barakah nuclear power plant (UAE, Al Dhafra, Abu Dhabi) drone attack May 17: three UAVs entered UAE from western border; two intercepted by UAE air defenses; one struck electrical generator outside inner perimeter; no radiation leak (IAEA T1 confirmation); one reactor on emergency diesel generators. IAEA statement confirmed (T1). No official attribution; UAE investigating. Western border entry points to Yemen (Houthi) or Iraqi militia origin. JPost sources (−50% discount): 'In any case, it's Iran who ordered the attack.' This is the first confirmed attack on UAE nuclear-adjacent infrastructure since the war began. The attack directly contradicts Cooper Tier-1 SASC testimony (May 14): 'Hamas, Hezbollah, and the Houthis are all cut off from Iran's weapons and support.' Reconciliation options: (a) Houthis have indigenous drone manufacturing capability (FP March 2026: local airframe/warhead assembly, guidance/propulsion still Iran-supplied) — not fully severed from Iranian supply chains; (b) Cooper's assessment was outdated within 8 days; (c) Iraqi militia (not Houthi) as launch platform, operating via Iran's Quds Force. All three scenarios indicate Iranian proxy network has residual capability not captured by Cooper's 'cut off' framing. Cooper Tier-1 claim discounted to M confidence on proxy connectivity. IRGC Zolfaghari Hormuz enforcement statement concurrent: maximalist Hormuz enforcement language ('did not allow any vessel') maintained while diplomatic exchange (revised text) occurs. Slantchev feigning-weakness: Barakah attack is an active use case of Iranian extended deterrent network, not merely residual inventory. First nuclear-adjacent infrastructure targeting by Iranian proxy network: new threshold crossed in the graduated escalation ladder.

finding_cards.probe_name: Mutual Dispositional Reading and Power-Shift Perception

sources.name: ABC News — 'Iran better get moving FAST; Trump says' May 18 2026
CNBC — 'Trump postponing scheduled attack; military prepared for full large-scale assault if deal not reached' May 18 2026
Times of Israel — 'US Iran said closing in on framework for permanent deal as Trump renews bomb threats' May 18 2026
Times of Israel — 'Netanyahu convenes security cabinet; prepared for any scenario' May 17 2026
SANA / Fox News — 'IRGC Zolfaghari: all US bases in southern Gulf will be deactivated' May 17 2026

finding_cards.finding: Trump dispositional state: maximum paradox. 'Won't be anything left of them if they don't get moving FAST' simultaneously with attack postponement framed as 'deal very close.' Trump's dispositional reading of Iran: both annihilatory ('nothing left') and open ('Iranians should be afraid' AND 'Iran still wants a deal'). This is A1 oscillation at its most extreme documented arc — not two readings separated by 48 hours but simultaneous contradictory frames in the same decision cycle. The Gulf state brake provides the structural explanation: Trump's dispositional preference may be hardline but external constraint (Gulf leaders' appeal + deal-proximity framing) produced postponement. Trump's decision-moment state: postponement = deal-leaning in this 48h window; 2-3 day clock = expiration back to hardline if no deal arrives. Netanyahu dispositional state: 'our eyes are also open; prepared for any scenario' — cautious formulation; engaging in trilateral diplomacy; not explicitly endorsing Trump postponement or pushing for immediate strike. IDF posture: Sulfur-and-Fire exercise (Day 79) + Zamir 'on high alert' framing persists; no update Day 80-81. IRGC dispositional reading: Zolfaghari 'all US bases will be deactivated' — maximum threat posture maintained even during diplomatic exchange. Iran's public stance: hardline threat + private diplomatic engagement = classic two-level game (Putnam). ToI: 'US and Iran closing in on framework for permanent deal as Trump renews bomb threats' — captures the paradox structurally. Gulf state troika dispositional reading: deal-seeking; Barakah attack on UAE infrastructure hardened their desire for diplomatic resolution over military escalation (being on the receiving end of Iranian deterrence signal motivates mediation, not counter-escalation).

De-escalatory architecture has deepened while kinetic signals have also intensified.

sources: Synthetic composite from PROBE-2, PROBE-7, PROBE-8, PROBE-9, PROBE-10, PROBE-12', PROBE-13, PROBE-14, PROBE-15 Day 81 outputs
finding_cards.finding: FRAMEWORK REVISION TRIGGER. Multiple first-mover thresholds reached simultaneously, producing the most compressed decision architecture the framework has seen. US-SIDE THRESHOLD: Operationally REACHED then PULLED BACK. Trump scheduled military strike for May 19; announced postponement May 18 at Gulf state request. The threshold was crossed (execution-ready state confirmed) and then reversed by external Gulf state constraint — the first documented case of Gulf states directly halting a US military action in this conflict. The 2-3 day window creates a formal expiration: absent deal signal by approximately May 20-21, execution-ready state can be reinstated. IRANIAN-SIDE THRESHOLD: Not breached; Ghalibaf-Araghchi track still diplomatic (revised text submitted); Vahidi silent. Strangulation threshold not reached (no Bazaar closure, rial stable). ISRAELI-SIDE THRESHOLD: Netanyahu in security cabinet + trilateral with Qatar PM; not driving independent pre-emption in this window; Knesset dissolution vote pending May 20-21. WPR-FORCING-FUNCTION: unchanged; attack postponement preserves constitutional window temporarily. STRANGULATION: Brent pulled back to $102 (deal signal softening kinetic premium); no Kharg saturation. NEW ARCHITECTURE ELEMENT: Gulf state troika (Saudi/UAE/Qatar) inserted as fourth structural actor in the first-mover decision space — not a principal in the Iran war framework, but an effective veto-by-appeal on US military timing. Their insertion directly interacted with the PROBE-14 finding: Barakah attack on UAE motivated UAE (and Saudi/Qatar solidarity) to appeal to Trump. Iranian deterrence signal (Barakah) paradoxically produced de-escalatory pressure on the US-Gulf side. Composite: the architecture is at maximum compression. The joint decision window (2-3 day Trump hold + Knesset dissolution vote May 20-21 + Hajj May 24-29) creates three concurrent forcing functions within a 7-day window. The first-mover question has partially resolved: the US moved (scheduled attack) and was braked; Iran responded (Barakah + revised text); Israel is holding (trilateral diplomacy); Gulf states are the new brake. What fires next — deal confirmation or military resumption — determines which fork actualizes.

trigger_detail.gap_to_full_match: N/A — FIRED. Framework convergence imminent within 2-3 day window.

Joint decision window: 2-3 days (Trump self-imposed holdoff) × Knesset vote (May 20-21) × Hajj (May 24-29). Fork C miscalculation risk elevated: multiple simultaneous threshold approaches with Barakah nuclear-adjacent attack adding accident-pathway risk. Revised first-mover architecture: five actors now in play — Iran, US, Israel, Gulf state troika, Pakistan mediator. The first-mover question has partially collapsed: the US was the first-mover (attack scheduled) and was braked externally. If the 2-3 day window expires without deal, US re-activates as first-mover. If deal is reached in window, Fork B-multilateral (Gulf state brokered) is the operative path.

proposed_revision.section: Section 5 Structural Dynamics (new §5.25 — Gulf State Troika Brake Mechanism); Section 3.4 Layer Four Faction Misalignment / Principal-Access-Channel Architecture; Section 8 Probability Matrix
proposed_revision.edit: Add §5.25 Gulf State Troika Brake Mechanism: Saudi/UAE/Qatar leaders (MBS, MBZ, Tamim) directly intervened in US military decision cycle on May 18, requesting 2-3 day postponement of attack scheduled for May 19. First confirmed case of Gulf state leaders halting a US military action timing in this conflict. The mechanism operates via: (a) Gulf state economic exposure to conflict (UAE's Barakah attack + oil-price political pressure on MBS/Tamim); (b) direct principal-level appeal to Trump A1 oscillation; (c) deal-optimism framing ('very close') that provides Trump cover for postponement. The brake is temporary (Trump's own 2-3 day framing) and conditional on deal progress. It is the first functional Fork B-multilateral mechanism in the Gulf state dimension; prior multilateral mechanisms were E3/IAEA and Beijing. Probability matrix: Fork B-multilateral (Gulf state pathway) elevated to 8-15% 30d (from sub-5% prior); combined Fork B 20-30% 30d (from 15-23%); Fork A 30d compressed 3-5pp on active brake mechanism; Fork D' marginally elevated if deal window expires without resolution.

Western-border drone origin (Yemen/Iraqi militia) demonstrates Houthi or Iraqi proxy capability not fully severed despite Cooper T1 claim. Layer 2 calibration: Iranian horizontal escalation ladder now includes nuclear-adjacent targeting as demonstrated tier — one step below direct attack on nuclear reactor (which IAEA safety norms and existential deterrence constrain). Cooper Tier-1 'cut off' assessment: apply −25% confidence discount; treat Houthi/Iraqi militia as retaining delivery capability via indigenous manufacturing and partial Iranian supply persistence. Update §1.4 Lebanon/Proxy Fronts: Houthi restraint from mass-launch should not be interpreted as capability severance. The Barakah attack is calibrated — generator outside inner perimeter, two of three drones intercepted — characteristic Slantchev feigning-weakness demonstration: capability signal without triggering maximum response.

calibration_check.assessment: Central thesis holding. The materialist bargaining model correctly predicted that constraint architecture would compose toward a kinetic option when all other paths were blocked — the US literally scheduled a military strike. The thesis also predicted that actors do not design outcomes but select within constraint surfaces: no actor designed the Gulf state troika brake mechanism; it emerged from Iranian deterrence signaling (Barakah) activating Gulf state economic exposure, which activated Gulf leader appeals to Trump A1 oscillation, which produced postponement. The framework did not model Gulf states as an operative brake on US military timing — this is the cycle's structural elaboration requirement. The five constraint layers hold: Layer 1 (military physics) bounded the scheduled strike to a discrete kinetic option, not sustained campaign; Layer 2 (asymmetric logic) produced the Barakah calibrated demonstration; Layer 3 (time arithmetic) compressed the decision window to 2-3 days; Layer 4 (faction misalignment) explains Gulf state troika as a parallel principal-access channel; Layer 5 (PA-gap) explains why Vahidi's silence and the Iranian revised-text submission coexist. The thesis does not break.
calibration_check.dominant_uncertainty: Whether the 2-3 day Trump holdoff (expiring approximately May 20-21) produces a confirmed deal framework or expires without resolution, triggering military resumption. This is the highest-acuity decision node the framework has seen. The second dominant uncertainty: Knesset dissolution vote (May 20-21) coincidence — passage restructures the Israeli decision-architecture at the worst possible timing from a Fork B-durability perspective. The third: whether Cooper's proxy-connectivity assessment was accurate and the Barakah attack represents autonomous Houthi capability vs. Iranian coordination — determines how to recalibrate Layer 2.
